title: Lecture Two Hall-Response / Chern-Number Dependency Graph
summary: Dependency snapshot for the Lecture Two flagship proof: level quantization precedes Hall current, the band invariant and many-body invariant are defined on their own geometric spaces, and the theorem closes only after those routes are fused.
assumptions: The proof order matters because later claims depend on earlier quantization and definition steps.
regime: Proof-order audit for the Hall-response theorem family.
scope: Ordered prerequisite spine for the Lecture Two theorem.
representation: Dependency snapshot for the Lecture Two proof order.
derivation_spine: Quantize the Chern-Simons level by the large-gauge argument on S^2 times S^1.
Differentiate the effective action to obtain the Hall current and identify k as the macroscopic response coefficient.
Define the microscopic band invariant k' from the Berry curvature of the filled-state bundle.
Derive the many-body invariant \widehat{k}' from the twist-angle Berry curvature.
Fuse the response, band, and many-body routes into k = k' = \widehat{k}'.
retrieval_hints: Use when an answer must respect the proof order and not jump to the final equality prematurely.
